activity:
08:00 – Metadata Curation and Database Maintenance  
Sits at the workstation in the WFPDB lab, surrounded by stacks of digitized plate scans and calibration logs. Begins updating metadata for 12 newly digitized photographic plates from the 1965–1972 Rozhen survey. Uses custom Python scripts to cross-reference plate headers (EXIF-like FITS headers from scanned negatives) with the WFPDB schema: verifying exposure time, telescope focal length, filter type (B, V, photographic emulsion), and astrometric reference stars. Corrects an error in the Right Ascension offset for Plate #RZ-7842, which had been misaligned due to a misrecorded telescope guide star. Adds a note to the plate’s “curatorial history” field: “Corrected RA offset via comparison with Tycho-2 catalog; verified by cross-match with DSS2.” Saves changes and triggers an automated checksum validation to ensure database integrity.
prompt: Why does the DSS2 cross-match sometimes resolve astrometric discrepancies that the Tycho-2 catalog misses, even though Tycho-2 is supposed to be more precise—could the digitization artifacts from 1960s emulsions be interacting with DSS2’s own calibration in unexpected ways?
